news.headline: Renters fear scams in tight housing market, 93% think scams are common, 90% worried about falling victim according to Rently survey.
news.summary: In Los Angeles, renters are facing increasing challenges in the competitive housing market due to a rise in sophisticated rental scams. A survey conducted by Rently revealed that 93% of renters consider scams to be a common occurrence, with 90% expressing concerns about becoming victims themselves. The 2024 Rently Survey: Rental Scams and Fraud Report, carried out by Pollfish, highlights the need for industry-wide measures to address this growing issue.

news.headline: Duolingo and Netflix collaborate for 'Learn Korean or Else' campaign before Squid Game S2; Duo the Owl & Pink Guard lead fans.
news.summary: A new addition to the Pink Guard has been made with the arrival of Duo the Owl, who will be teaming up with the group to engage fans in various challenges. This partnership brings excitement as Duo the Owl joins the Pink Guard in their fan interactions. The Pink Guard now stands strengthened with this collaboration, ready to take on challenges and engage with their audience. Stay tuned to see how this new development adds a fun and engaging element to the fan experience.

news.headline: IBM introduces co-packaged optics for data centers, boosting speed and energy efficiency for AI, computing apps.
news.summary: In a recent development, IBM has introduced a groundbreaking co-packaged optics technology that has the potential to supplant electrical interconnects within data centers. This innovation, presented in Yorktown Heights, New York on December 10, 2024, aims to deliver substantial enhancements in speed and energy efficiency for various computing tasks, particularly beneficial for AI applications. With IBM (NYSE: IBM) at the forefront, this advancement marks a significant step towards transformative improvements in data center operations.

news.headline: Gran Tierra Energy Inc. priced $150 million of 9.500% Senior Secured Amortizing Notes due 2029 in private placement.
news.summary: Gran Tierra Energy Inc. revealed the pricing of an additional $150 million aggregate principal amount for its previously issued 9.500% Senior Secured Amortizing Notes due 2029. The offering was made to qualified institutional buyers in the United States under Rule 144A of the Securities Act of 1933, as well as to non-U.S. persons outside the U.S. under Regulation S of the Securities Act, and through specific prospectus exemptions in Canada. The company's stock is listed on multiple exchanges, including NYSE American, TSX, and LSE.

news.headline: Global Jet Capital raises $617M with 8th ABS offering, totaling $5.8B assets securitized and $4.8B bonds issued.
news.summary: On September 17, 2024, in Danbury, CT, Global Jet Capital, a prominent provider of financial services for business aircraft, revealed the finalization of its BJETS 2024-2 securitization. This initiative gathered around $617 million, marking Global Jet Capital's eighth asset-backed security (ABS) endeavor. Overall, the organization has now secured close to $5.8 billion in assets and issued about $4.8 billion in bonds.

news.headline: Local bank to become region's biggest with upcoming merger.
Hepsiburada announces second issuance of asset-backed securities worth TRY 350 million on Sept. 30, 2024.
news.summary: Today, a prominent Turkish e-commerce platform, D-MARKET Electronic Services & Trading known as "Hepsiburada," revealed the launch of its second asset-backed securities totaling TRY 350 million. The announcement was made in Istanbul on Sept. 30, 2024.

news.headline: Eterna Therapeutics finalizes financial restructuring to boost developmental activities for long-term success.
news.summary: Eterna Therapeutics has successfully finalized a strategic financial restructuring process to enhance its developmental endeavors and ensure long-term prosperity. This move aims to propel the company's growth and innovation, positioning it for success in the future. With this restructuring, Eterna Therapeutics looks to leverage its resources more effectively and efficiently to drive its activities forward. This strategic financial realignment will play a crucial role in shaping the company's trajectory, and underscores its commitment to creating a sustainable and impactful presence in the industry.

news.headline: Azerion files for EUR 50 million bond issuance on Nasdaq Stockholm, excluding distribution in US or restricted jurisdictions.
news.summary: This document is not intended for distribution to any individual in the United States or to any U.S. person, as defined in Regulation S under the U.S. Securities Act of 1933, or in any jurisdiction where such distribution is unlawful.

news.headline: NowCM and White & Case set up a digital, fully automated commercial paper program for Haniel - a global first.
Trump selects Rep. Lori Chavez-DeRemer for Labor Secretary after endorsements from unions. Lost bid for 2nd term narrowly.
news.summary: A politician from a swing district, she gained support from multiple unions but narrowly missed securing a second term in the House of Representatives.

news.headline: Matinas BioPharma terminates MAT2203 partnership negotiations, leading to an 80% workforce reduction with 15 positions cut.
news.summary: In Bedminster, New Jersey on October 31, 2024, Matinas BioPharma Holdings, Inc. revealed the end of negotiations for global rights to MAT2203, an oral amphotericin B formulation, after the potential partner's withdrawal. Consequently, Matinas has initiated an immediate 80% reduction in workforce, cutting 15 positions, including three senior management roles. Furthermore, all product development efforts have been halted to preserve funds.

news.headline: Trump is known for exempting preferred firms from tariffs. Companies are seeking to sway him as they await his decisions.
news.summary: Donald Trump has a history of pardoning favored companies from tariffs, with reports showing an increase in companies vying to sway his decisions. This trend is exemplified by the number of companies seeking forgiveness, which has risen sharply in recent months. Trump's penchant for granting reprieves from tariffs has created an environment where businesses are eager to lobby for their own exemptions. The growing number of companies seeking clemency underscores the influence Trump wields in this domain.

news.headline: Guggenheim anticipates mixed effects of Fed rate cuts on high yield bonds in Q4 2024.
news.summary: The impact of interest rate reductions on high yield bonds could yield varying results. This can be attributed to the fluctuating nature of high yield bond performance in light of interest rate adjustments. The relationship between rate cuts and high yield bonds is complex, with outcomes ranging from potential profit gains to risks of increased default rates. Investors should be mindful of these dynamics as they consider the implications of rate cuts on high yield bonds.

news.headline: Verve Therapeutics grants equity awards to one new employee on November 29, 2024, under Nasdaq Listing Rule 5635(c)(4).
news.summary: On November 29, 2024, Verve Therapeutics in Boston revealed that they provided equity awards to one fresh staff member under the 2024 Inducement Stock Incentive Plan. This move was crucial in persuading the new employee to join the company and aligns with Nasdaq Listing Rule 5635(c)(4). Verve Therapeutics focuses on developing a new category of genetic medications for cardiovascular disease and is currently in the clinical stage.

news.headline: Brookfield plans corporate structure changes for shareholder ownership, vote on Dec. 20, 2024.
news.summary: BAM shareholders will cast their votes during a special meeting scheduled for December 20, 2024. The shareholders will convene on this date to make crucial decisions regarding the company's future. This meeting will play a significant role in determining the direction of BAM, with important resolutions and proposals set to be discussed and voted upon. It marks a pivotal moment for the company as stakeholders come together to exercise their voting rights and shape the path forward.

news.headline: Pineapple Energy shareholders approve name change to SUNation Energy, Inc. and move to Delaware incorporation.
news.summary: Pineapple Energy has revealed a significant update, indicating a shift in its corporate identity to SUNation Energy, Inc. Additionally, the company has altered its official state of incorporation to Delaware. This decision marks an important transition for the company, signifying a new chapter in its operations and growth prospects.

news.headline: Will Wall Street billionaires fulfill Trump's promise to blue-collar workers? concern over their commitment.
news.summary: The incoming president has appointed affluent individuals to critical economic roles, prompting speculation on their commitment to aiding the working class.

news.headline: Navigator Gas placed USD 100 million of senior unsecured bonds in Nordic market, with fixed 7.25% coupon, maturing in 2029.
news.summary: In London on October 17, 2024, Navigator Holdings Ltd. (referred to as "Navigator Gas" or the "Company") (NYSE: NVGS) confirmed the placement of USD 100 million in new senior unsecured bonds in the Nordic bond market. These bonds, set to mature in October 2029, will carry a fixed coupon rate of 7.25% per year, with payments made semi-annually in arrears. Navigator Gas maintains the world's largest fleet of handysize liquefied gas carriers.

news.headline: Biden reduces Intel's chip funding by over $600M post winning $3B military contract & adjusting investment plans.
news.summary: Following the company's success in securing a $3 billion military contract and altering its investment pledges, it is anticipated to receive reduced funding from the CHIPS Act. The Silicon Valley organization had originally been slated to receive a higher amount from the Act, but the revised figures reflect the company's updated financial situation and changed priorities.

news.headline: Rocky Mountain High Brands announces management restructuring and new initiatives to optimize capabilities and business performance.
news.summary: In Plano, Texas on November 8, 2024, Rocky Mountain High Brands, Inc. (OTC: RMHB) revealed a managerial overhaul aimed at enhancing the Company's operations and overall business efficiency. This decision underscores a pivotal moment in the Company's expansion and sets the stage for a redefined future.

news.headline: Mexican president considers imposing counter tariffs amid threat from President-elect Trump, warning of economic harm to both nations.
news.summary: President Claudia Sheinbaum commented on President-elect Trump's warning of imposing substantial tariffs, stating that such a action would harm both nations.

news.headline: PIMCO Canada announces details for merging 3 funds into a new one managed by them: PTI, PTO, and PIX into PMEI.
news.summary: On October 18, 2024, PIMCO Canada Corp. revealed additional information regarding the proposed mergers of PIMCO Tactical Income Fund (PTI), PIMCO Tactical Income Opportunities Fund (PTO), and PIMCO Multi-Sector Income Fund (PIX) into a new closed-end fund named PIMCO Monthly Enhanced Income Fund (PMEI). The merger aims to consolidate the Existing Funds under the management of PIMCO Canada. This move indicates a strategic shift in the company's investment approach, potentially streamlining operations and offering enhanced income opportunities to investors.

news.headline: 23andMe plans to restructure its business to streamline operations, cut costs, and prepare for the future.
news.summary: In a recent report, it was highlighted that the unemployment rate in the country has decreased to 4.2%, marking a significant drop from the previous month. The data revealed that the economy added 250,000 new jobs in various sectors, surpassing analysts' expectations. Additionally, the report indicated that the average hourly wage saw an increase of 3.5% compared to last year. These promising figures suggest a positive trend in the labor market, providing hope for economic growth and stability in the foreseeable future.

news.headline: AIFXL revolutionizes forex trading with The Calculus AI technology, offering retail traders elite tools. Leading change in industry.
news.summary: In London on December 1, 2024, AIFXL is leading a transformation in the forex trading sector with its revolutionary AI trading tool, The Calculus. This innovative solution can analyze extensive real-time data and predict market patterns, granting retail traders access to tools typically exclusive to prominent institutions. Consequently, AIFXL's approach has resulted in steady profits, advanced risk control, and a fundamental change in trading practices.

news.headline: U.S. trade representative warns of shift away from free trade under Trump presidency.
news.summary: According to a former assistant U.S. trade representative, the election of Donald Trump would expedite a departure from the longstanding free trade agreement of the U.S.

news.headline: Minutes from a recent meeting revealed Fed considering gradual interest rate cuts after Nov. 6-7 meeting.
news.summary: During a meeting held on November 6-7, minutes revealed that members of the Federal Reserve expressed a preference for a gradual decrease in rates.

news.headline: PVH Corp. (NYSE:PVH) Q3 sales hit $2.255B, topping estimates. International sales flat, N.A. down 6%, D2C flat. PVH Corp's Calvin Klein misses guidance, hitting the stock.
news.summary: PVH Corp. trading on NYSE as PVH saw a decline in shares post-reporting of Q3 results with weak guidance. The sales for the quarter were $2.255 billion, surpassing the analyst consensus of $2.220 billion. International business revenues remained stagnant YoY and decreased by 2% on a constant currency basis. Growth in Asia Pacific was balanced by reduced sales in Europe to enhance sales quality. North America experienced a 6% drop in revenue from Tommy Hilfiger and Calvin Klein brands, partly due to shifted wholesale shipments. Direct-to-consumer revenue remained flat YoY (declined by 1% in constant currency). Owned store revenue rose by 1% YoY, while digital commerce revenue saw a decrease.

news.headline: Mercer International Inc. prices private offering of $200.0 million in senior notes at 12.875% due 2028.
news.summary: Mercer International Inc. disclosed on October 23, 2024, the pricing details of its private offering. The company, listed as MERC on Nasdaq, announced the issuance of $200.0 million worth of 12.875% senior notes due on October 1, 2028, under the name "Additional Notes."

news.headline: Trump's pledge to impose tariffs on Canada, Mexico, and China disrupts global trade, imperiling agreements he made in office.
news.summary: The president-elect's warning of imposing new tariffs on Canada, Mexico, and China has sent shockwaves through business and diplomatic realms. This action could potentially disrupt the trade agreements he had previously established during his first term in office.

news.headline: Beyond Oil expands into US market with 3 customers piloting their solution, appoints Jason Hatfield to lead US sales.
news.summary: Beyond Oil is expanding its presence globally with three customers testing the company's innovative solution, marking its entry into the U.S food service market. This development underscores the company's progress and increasing influence in the industry.

news.headline: Greystone Management Solutions rebrands to Greystone Real Estate Advisory Group after 25 years, offering expanded services since 1999.
news.summary: In New York on November 12, 2024, Greystone Management Solutions, a company known for offering top-tier commercial real estate management services to the public sector for 25 years, has rebranded as the Greystone Real Estate Advisory Group. The rebranding is effective immediately and marks the evolution of the division’s services and resources since its inception in 1999.

news.headline: Business groups across various sectors want Congress to limit president’s ability to impose new tariffs.
news.summary: Leaders from a diverse group of technology companies, manufacturers, retailers, and agricultural producers are urging Congress to establish measures that would limit a president's power to enact additional tariffs. The collective effort highlights the importance of implementing safeguards to protect the economy and industries from potential negative impacts.

news.headline: Tariff threat by President Trump causing tension between Canada and Mexico, raising concerns about the future of North American relations.
news.summary: The new president's initial moves in trade and border negotiations with neighboring countries are putting significant pressure on the North American alliance.

news.headline: PIMCO declares monthly distributions for closed-end funds, payable on Dec 2, 2024, to shareholders of record on Nov 12.
news.summary: On November 1, 2024, the Boards of Trustees/Directors of various PIMCO closed-end funds announced monthly distributions for their common shares. The distributions will be paid on December 2, 2024, to shareholders recorded by November 12, 2024, with an ex-dividend date of November 12, 2024.

news.headline: US stocks set to open weak as Bitcoin hits $103,000. Jobless data eyed, BlackRock sees 'US Exceptionalism' in 2025. Numbers: Nasdaq -0.10%, S&P -0.02%, Dow -0.01%.
news.summary: The U.S. stock market is expected to open weak on Thursday following record highs on Wednesday. Futures for all three major indices were slightly down, indicating cautious sentiment on Wall Street. Federal Reserve chair Jerome Powell emphasized a strong economy and uncertainty about inflation, downplaying expectations of swift rate cuts. Despite this, expectations for a 25-basis-point rate cut in December stand at 74%. Bitcoin surged past $103,000 for the first time. In premarket trading, the SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust fell by 0.03% to $607.46, while the Invesco QQQ ETF declined by 0.10% to $522.72. On Wednesday, all three major indices closed positively, with the Dow Jones surpassing 45,000 for the first time.

news.headline: EyePoint Pharmaceuticals granted non-statutory stock options to new employees outside 2023 Incentive Plan. NASDAQ: EYPT.
news.summary: In Watertown, Massachusetts on November 18, 2024, EyePoint Pharmaceuticals, Inc. (NASDAQ: EYPT) revealed granting non-statutory stock options to new employees as inducement awards. The company focuses on creating and marketing therapeutics to enhance the quality of life for patients dealing with severe retinal diseases. These options were provided outside the Company’s 2023 Long-Term Incentive Plan in agreement with NASDAQ Listing Rule 5635(c)(4).

news.headline: Curaleaf Holdings (OTC:CURLF) stock has dropped from $5.50 to $1.70 in 2023. Facing industry challenges, it is down 75% from 2022 high of $8.
news.summary: The article discusses the stock performance of Curaleaf Holdings Inc. (OTC:CURLF), the first cannabis stock traded over the counter. Despite being a major player in the U.S. cannabis industry, CURLF has experienced a significant downtrend, dropping from $5.50 in early 2023 to around $1.70 currently. This represents a 75% drop from its peak of over $8 in 2022. Unlike other OTC stocks, CURLF maintains sound trading volumes. The decline is attributed to industry challenges and regulatory constraints affecting growth and investor confidence. The stock seems yet to find the bottom, unlike other cannabis stocks that are recovering in April-May 2024.

news.headline: Hurricane Helene causes surge in unemployment claims in North Carolina, particularly impacting the western part of the state.
news.summary: In the aftermath of Hurricane Helene hitting the western region of North Carolina, the state is currently experiencing a significant influx of unemployment insurance claims. The surge in claims is putting pressure on the system as thousands of individuals are seeking assistance due to the impact of the natural disaster. The exact number of claims has not been disclosed, but officials are addressing the overwhelming volume of applications to provide support to those affected by the hurricane's destruction.

news.headline: Empire Communities Corp. closed CAD$225 million Senior Notes offering, redeeming 7.375% Senior Notes. New Notes: 7.625% due Nov 1, 2029.
news.summary: Located in Vaughan, Ontario, on November 4, 2024, Empire Communities Corp. disclosed the completion of a private placement offering totaling CAD$225 million in 7.625% senior unsecured notes due by November 1, 2029. The Notes were sold at par value and are supported by specific Empire subsidiaries. Interest payments on the Notes will occur semi-annually on May 1 and November 1 starting from May 1, 2025.

news.headline: ATA Creativity Global regains compliance with Nasdaq's minimum bid price, but receives notification about minimum stockholders' equity deficiency. Dec 03, 2024. AACG.
news.summary: Beijing, Dec. 03, 2024 - ATA Creativity Global, known as ACG and listed on Nasdaq as AACG, a global educational services firm emphasizing quality learning to nurture student creativity, shared key compliance updates on meeting Nasdaq's listing rules.

news.headline: Union negotiator "Su" on verge of securing third consecutive win with 98% member support.
SIMPPLE Ltd. partners with Elite VDP for Vietnam expansion. Boosting distribution of SIMPPLE Ecosystem.
news.summary: In Singapore on December 3, 2024, SIMPPLE Ltd. (NASDAQ: SPPL), a notable technology provider in facilities management, revealed a significant partnership development. The Company's Singapore branch secured a deal with Elite VDP Co., Ltd. in Vietnam to distribute the SIMPPLE Ecosystem throughout the country. This strategic partnership marks a noteworthy advancement for SIMPPLE's expansion, highlighting its commitment to reaching new markets.

news.headline: US steel industry expected to be protected regardless of election outcome due to importance in swing states like Pennsylvania.
news.summary: Whoever emerges victorious in the approaching presidential election, the U.S. steel sector is poised to receive ongoing support due to its significant role in pivotal swing states such as Pennsylvania. The industry is a crucial sector in these states, contributing substantially to their economies and employing tens of thousands of workers. As a result, regardless of the election outcome, the steel industry is expected to be safeguarded to maintain its economic and political importance.

news.headline: Jamieson Greer chosen by Trump as Trade Representative. Key role as U.S. trade negotiator amid talk of tariffs.
news.summary: Jamieson Greer, an experienced lawyer, is appointed as the primary U.S. trade negotiator, a pivotal role amidst the president-elect's plan to potentially levy tariffs on foreign imports.

news.headline: Hepsiburada's subsidiary, Hepsifinans, announces second bond issuance at TRY 150 million.
news.summary: On November 6, 2024, Hepsiburada, a prominent Turkish e-commerce platform operating as D-MARKET Electronic Services & Trading, disclosed the launch of the second bond issuance by its subsidiary Hepsi Finansman A.Ş. The nominal value of this issuance stands at TRY 150 million._owned subsidiary, Hepsi Finansman A.Ş.

news.headline: Trump administration prepares for major trade overhaul, significant changes expected.
news.summary: With a surge in COVID-19 cases, the state reported a record-breaking 5,000 new cases. Hospitalizations have also increased by 30%, reaching a total of 1,200 patients. The positivity rate has climbed to 15%, alarming health officials. In response, the governor has implemented stricter restrictions and urges the public to adhere to safety guidelines to curb the spread of the virus.

news.headline: Traders await November jobs report for Fed's interest rate cut decision. Market odds suggest 70% chance of 25-basis-point cut. Consensus estimates project November nonfarm payroll growth at 200,000, with previous month skewed due to disruptions. Fed Governor noted 100,000-120,000 jobs missing from October may show in November. ADP report provides slight unclear outlook on private payrolls.
news.summary: Traders are focusing on November's jobs data release this week, a crucial factor in the Federal Reserve's decision on a potential rate cut. Market odds indicate a 70% chance for a 25-basis-point reduction, with November's nonfarm payroll growth expected to rebound sharply from October. October saw just 12,000 jobs added, influenced by hurricanes and strikes. Economists anticipate November's nonfarm payroll growth to hit 200,000, suggesting stronger job growth as the impacts of weather and strikes diminish. Federal Reserve Governor Adriana Kugler suggested that October's disruptions may have led to 100,000 to 120,000 unaccounted jobs, which could surface in November. Private payrolls data from the ADP National Employment Report for November appeared less definitive, using data from around 25 million employees.

news.headline: Eyenovia in restructuring effort, gets loan deferral from Avenue Capital until Feb 2025.
news.summary: A deal has been struck between Avenue Capital and the Company, allowing for the deferment of principal and interest payments on the Company's existing debt. This agreement stipulates that payments will be deferred until February 2025.

news.headline: SurgeonCheck launches website for surgeon recommendations based on 10.3 billion claims and 148 million procedures.
news.summary: In Bethlehem, SurgeonCheck unveiled a revolutionary surgeon recommendation tool today. By visiting www.surgeoncheck.com, patients can easily find personalized suggestions for top surgeons in their vicinity. The recommendations stem from an extensive review of clinical data sourced from 10.3 billion claims and 148 million procedures. Updates on recommendations are made every six months through data refreshes. Previously, patients commonly depended on referrals for such decisions.
